(b)        the representative of the person or organisation.


8.10     This paragraph applies to service of an application for variation or discharge of a DAPO on notice as it applies to service of an application for a DAPO on notice.


(Rule 6.35 FPR enables the court to direct that service is effected by an alternative method or at an alternative place and rule 6.36 FPR enables the court to dispense with service.)


## 9. Service of application for a DAPO without notice to P


9.1       This paragraph applies where an application for a DAPO without notice to P is made by—


(a)        the person to be protected; or


(b)        an applicant who has been granted permission by the court to apply for a DAPO under section 28(2)(d) of the 2021 Act.


9.2       The applicant must serve the following persons with the documents listed in paragraph 9.3—


(a)        any person directed by the court (where the applicant is the person to be protected); and


(b)        where the applicant has been granted permission by the court to apply for a DAPO under section 28(2)(d) of the 2021 Act—


(i)         the person to be protected; and


(ii)        any other person directed by the court,


not less than 2 days before the date on which the application will be heard.


9.3       The documents that must be served on the persons listed in paragraph 9.2 are—


(a)        a copy of the DAPO application and supporting statement;


(b)        notice of the hearing; and


(c)        the notice of information for the person to be protected, if required by paragraph 8.7.


9.4       The court may abridge the period specified in paragraph 9.2.


9.5       Where the DAPO application relates to a property and sets out details of any mortgagee or landlord of the property pursuant to paragraph 3.4(c)(ii), the applicant must serve on the mortgagee or landlord—


(a)        a copy of the application; and


(b)        notice of the right to make representations in writing.


9.6       The documents under paragraphs 9.3 and 9.5 must be served in accordance with the relevant provisions of paragraph 8.
